texts.label: text
texts.orig: Acid gas removal from natural gas is one of the major processes in a gas treatment plant [1]. Presence of CO2 and H2S in natural gas cause corrosion of pipelines and reduce the heating capacity of natural gas. In addition, the presence of the acid gases in the flue gas of power plants leads to pollution of the environment. The different techniques such as physical and chemical absorption are used for gas purification so that due to highly reactive nature and low cost of the alkanolamines, these solvents are widely used as a chemical absorbent for acid gas removal [2]. There are various alkanolamines with the differing properties and specifications such as monoethanolamine (MEA), diethanolamine (DEA), N-methyldiethanolamine (MDEA), diisopropanolamine (DIPA) so that each alkanolamine has own advantages and disadvantages. The common alkanolamines are MEA and DEA as primary and secondary amines, respectively, in which they are corrosive and present high absorption capacity, but they produce carbamate in reaction with CO2 and amine salts so that high thermal energy is needed for regeneration. In gas treatment plants, DIPA as a secondary alkanolamine is used in ADIP, sulfinol and SCOT processes

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Removal of hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide from sour gas is important in the natural gas treatment process and capturing of acid gases from flue gases. Moreover, for the design and construction of gas contactor equipment, experimental data of the solubility of H2S and CO2 in aqueous alkanolamines are needed. In this work using a volumetric static method, solubility of H2S in aqueous blends of Nmethyldiethanolamine (MDEA), 2-amino-2methyl-1-propanol (AMP) and piperazine (Pz) with different compositions are obtained at 313.15, 328.15 and 343.15 K over the pressure range of 1-21 bar. The composition of the MDEA is fixed at 25 mass % and the composition of the AMP and Pz are changed as (20, 15, 10, 5) mass % and (0, 5, 10, 15) mass %, respectively, so that the sum of mass fractions of all amines (MDEA + AMP + PZ) is kept constant. The experimental data are presented as the partial pressure of H2S against acid gas loading (moles H2S/total moles of amine). Considering the present results at the given conditions, it is observed that in the gas loading region less than one, blending Pz with an aqueous solution of the MDEA + AMP leads to reduction of absorption of the H2S.
texts.text: Removal of hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide from sour gas is important in the natural gas treatment process and capturing of acid gases from flue gases. Moreover, for the design and construction of gas contactor equipment, experimental data of the solubility of H2S and CO2 in aqueous alkanolamines are needed. In this work using a volumetric static method, solubility of H2S in aqueous blends of Nmethyldiethanolamine (MDEA), 2-amino-2methyl-1-propanol (AMP) and piperazine (Pz) with different compositions are obtained at 313.15, 328.15 and 343.15 K over the pressure range of 1-21 bar. The composition of the MDEA is fixed at 25 mass % and the composition of the AMP and Pz are changed as (20, 15, 10, 5) mass % and (0, 5, 10, 15) mass %, respectively, so that the sum of mass fractions of all amines (MDEA + AMP + PZ) is kept constant. The experimental data are presented as the partial pressure of H2S against acid gas loading (moles H2S/total moles of amine). Considering the present results at the given conditions, it is observed that in the gas loading region less than one, blending Pz with an aqueous solution of the MDEA + AMP leads to reduction of absorption of the H2S.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: [3]. MDEA is a tertiary amine that presents no carbamate formation in absorption of CO2 so that its most striking feature is the selective removal of H2S from sour gases. In the recent years, sterically hindered amines have been considered, because of their high absorption capacity and high absorption rate [4]. A sterically hindered amine presents a type of structure that could be a primary or secondary amine in which an amine group is attached to the ternary carbon or a secondary carbon, respectively. The 2-amino2methyl-1-propanol (AMP) is the most known sterically amine that is a kind of the hindered form of MEA. Moreover, in the recent years the chemical additive as an activator such as piperazine is blended with the alkanolamines to improve CO2 adsorption capacity of the amines [5]. Moreover to reduce the corrosion, the piperazine as a secondary type of the alkanolamine is often used as a chemical activator with MEA and DEA [6]. As a result, presently using a formulated blend of alkanolamines with a chemical activator allows one to pronounce selectivity and intensify the simultaneous absorption of both CO2 and H2S from sour gases so that the solubility data of acid gases in the blended alkanolamines has received highly demanded.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: H2S in the mono alkanolamine is more available than those solubility data that have been measured for the mixed alkanolamines. For example, a lot of data on the solubility of H2S in MDEA in the wide range of temperatures and pressures are given in the literature as are summarized in Table 1 while less works have been carried out for measurements of the hydrogen sulfide in the blended MDEA and the other alkanolamines. For the mixed-alkanolamines, Xia et al. [19] obtained the data for solubility of H2S in the Pz + MDEA solution and Rebolledo-Libreros et al. [20] measured the solubility of H2S in the MDEA + DEA + AMP mixture. They concluded that the aqueous MDEA + DEA + AMP system is highly selective towards H2S under the given conditions of pressure and temperature. MacGregor and Mather [10] investigated the solubility of H2S in a mixed-aqueous solvents consisting of MDEA and sulfolane so that their results were compared with those data given for the aqueous AMP + sulfolane system. They founded that the activated MDEA system presents a higher cyclic capacity than the activated AMP at high acid gas partial pressures (>1000 kPa). Finally, Li and Chang [21] measured the solubility of H2S in the aqueous mixtures of MEA and AMP.

texts.orig: In this work, the solubility of H2S in the aqueous MDEA + AMP + Pz system is measured using a static high pressure system. The data are measured at fixed 45 mass % of total amine so that the solubility of H2S in the present system is investigated under isothermal conditions at 313.15, 328.15 and 343.15 K and in the pressure range of 1-21 bar. In addition, the measurement of the H2S solubility is performed for the aqueous AMP + Pz (15 + 5) mass % system.

texts.orig: N-methyldiethanolamine and Pz with mass fraction purity >0.99 were supplied from Sigma-Aldrich and 2-amino-2methyl1-propanol with mass fraction purity >0.97 was purchased from Fluke. The H2S gas cylinder was supplied by Technical Gas Service Company with the mole fraction purity of 99.99%. All the materials were used without further purification. A digital balance with accuracy of ± 0.001 g was used to weigh the amines and water. The deionized-degassed water was used to make the present aqueous alkanolamines systems.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The solubility data are obtained through pressure measurement using a quasi-static high pressure apparatus as shown in Fig. 1. The experimental setup consists of an equilibrium cell made of Hastelloy C22/C276 with a capacity of about 285 ml; tolerate pressure up to 350 bar and temperature up to 300 ◦ C. The cell is equipped with a magnetic stirrer for agitation of the liquid phase and a 316 stainless steel storage tank with capacity about 245 ml that is used for injection of the acid gases into the equilibrium cell. The gas cylinders connect to the storage cell and a precise thermostatic circulator is used to keep the cell isothermally at a given temperature. The temperature and pressure of the gas-liquid equilibrium sample were measured in the cell, respectively, by a platinum temperature sensor (pt 100) and the 0-40 bar calibrated pressure transducer (model 33X, Keller, Switzerland) with accuracy of ± 0.0175% of full scale. For the injection cell a calibrated thermocouple thermometer (model ST-612, Standard Instrument Co. Ltd., Hong Kong) with accuracy of ± 0.1 K and (0-100) bar calibrated pressure transducer (model 33X, Keller, Switzerland) with accuracy of ± 0.017% of full scale were used. The vapor pressure of the aqueous alkanolamines systems was measured through a 0-1 bar pressure transducer (model PTF106, Indumart, Canada) with accuracy of ± 0.3% of full scale. The temperature of the equilibrium cell was controlled within ± 0.01 K using a highly responsive circulator temperature controller (unistat tango nuevo, Büchi AG, Switzerland). The cell is evacuated by means of a vacuum pump (model DV-142N-PLATINUM 5 CFM, JB company, USA) so that a monitoring unit (bds mc, Büchi AG, Switzerland) and a personal computer are used to receiving and storing all of the data from the measurement devices.

texts.orig: The solubility data are produced through the measurement of the gas pressure that is monitored using the static gas-liquid equilibrium method. Initially, the deionized water is degassed by employing a water-filled ultrasonic bath (model WUC-D10H, DAIHAN Scientific Co. Ltd., Korea) and consequently, the alkanolamines mixture is weighed using a digital balance to prepare the certain amount of the aqueous solution of amines. About 100 ml of the aqueous amine mixture is introduced into the reactor cell in which the solution is degassed using a vacuum pump and temperature of the pressure equilibrium cell is brought to the desired temperature by a thermostatic circulator. At this stage, the vapor pressure of the amine solution is evaluated at the given temperature and then H2S is injected into the reactor cell through the injection cell. The magnetic stirrer is turning on so that the gas-liquid equilibrium is achieved and no change in equilibrium pressure ( Pt ) of the cell is observed after about 30 min. The number of moles of the injected acid gas ( n H2 S ) is calculated using Peng-Robinson EOS by monitoring the values of the pressure and temperature before and after injection. texts.label: text
texts.orig: Using a quasi-static equilibrium method, the solubility of H2S in the different aqueous alkanolamine systems was obtained through the gas-liquid equilibrium pressure measurements. The systems of the AMP + Pz (15 + 5) mass %, MDEA + AMP (25 + 20) mass %, MDEA + AMP + Pz (25 + 15 + 5) mass %, MDEA + AMP + Pz (25 + 10 + 10) mass % and MDEA + AMP + Pz (25 + 5 + 15) mass % were investigated at 313.15, 328.15 and 343.15 K and in pressure range of (1-21) bar. The experimental results were presented as the partial pressure of H2S against the acid gas loading. For all of the aqueous alkanolamines systems containing Pz with increasing temperature the solubility of H2S was reduced at a fixed partial pressure. Pz can be used as a chemical activator by introducing to the MDEA or MDEA + AMP systems so that the selectivity to CO2 was intensified. However in this work, one can consider the selectivity to H2S was partially reduced through blending Pz with MDEA + AMP at low acid gas loading, but at high gas loading the absorption of H2S was intensified.
texts.text: Using a quasi-static equilibrium method, the solubility of H2S in the different aqueous alkanolamine systems was obtained through the gas-liquid equilibrium pressure measurements. The systems of the AMP + Pz (15 + 5) mass %, MDEA + AMP (25 + 20) mass %, MDEA + AMP + Pz (25 + 15 + 5) mass %, MDEA + AMP + Pz (25 + 10 + 10) mass % and MDEA + AMP + Pz (25 + 5 + 15) mass % were investigated at 313.15, 328.15 and 343.15 K and in pressure range of (1-21) bar. The experimental results were presented as the partial pressure of H2S against the acid gas loading. For all of the aqueous alkanolamines systems containing Pz with increasing temperature the solubility of H2S was reduced at a fixed partial pressure. Pz can be used as a chemical activator by introducing to the MDEA or MDEA + AMP systems so that the selectivity to CO2 was intensified. However in this work, one can consider the selectivity to H2S was partially reduced through blending Pz with MDEA + AMP at low acid gas loading, but at high gas loading the absorption of H2S was intensified.

texts.orig: For modeling the solubility of H2S in the blended alkanolamines, the Electrolyte-NRTL activity coefficient equation was applied to correlation and prediction of the partial pressure of H2S versus the acid gas loading. The absolute average deviation percent presented that the present modeling was successful in correlation and prediction of binary, ternary, quaternary and five component alkanolamine systems.
